title: ficus pedunculosa var. segaarensis
intro: Ficus pedunculosa var. segaarensis, also known as the Segaarensis or Saka tree, is a plant species within the Moraceae family. It is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Madagascar, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Ficus pedunculosa var. segaarensis are said to include its ability to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to act as an anti-inflammatory agent. Herbal preparations of Ficus pedunculosa var. segaarensis include infusions and decoctions, often made from its bark, leaves, or roots, which are used to treat various ailments.
